title: Build a JSX review toolbar on top of the integrated working set
summary: Introduced a review toolbar that turns the integrated JSX working set into a real triage surface, with session-driven actions clustered at the top of the board.
goal: Use the integrated working set as the base for a denser React review toolbar that mirrors how frontend teams handle queues in practice.
changesMade: Added a top-level review toolbar above the task list
Grouped shared actions so the board feels more like a review console
Aligned toolbar behavior with selection and filter state
Updated the fixture narrative to connect the toolbar with prior JSX sessions

features.name: review toolbar
features.why: Once the JSX board has multiple actions, those actions need a coherent home that reflects review intent.

features.name: integrated action grouping
features.why: Bulk, filter, and review flows should feel like one system instead of separate patches to the same file.

features.name: story continuity update
features.why: The handoff story should now show a path from isolated JSX features to a cohesive review experience.

decisions.decision: Place the review toolbar above the filtered list
decisions.why: Shared actions should reflect the visible working set, not sit far away from the content they affect.

decisions.decision: Keep the toolbar declarative and local to App.jsx
decisions.why: The fixture stays easier to reason about if the whole JSX interaction remains in one file.

openQuestions: Should the toolbar preview how many hidden selected rows exist when search is active?
resumePrompt: Continue the review toolbar in fixtures/react-todo-conflict/src/App.jsx and keep the toolbar synchronized with both filter and selection state.
